meta.description: Builds background-gap-objective logic for biomedical manuscript introductions with clear study positioning and disciplined narrative structure.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated literature, PMIDs, guidelines, or consensus positions detected. Hard rules prohibit inventing gaps or strengthening contributions beyond input.

practice_boundaries.detail: No clinical recommendations produced. Skill explicitly prohibits overstating study contributions.

methodological_ground.detail: No methodological fallacies. Study positioning rules enforce evidence-level accuracy.

code_usability.detail: No code generated; Mode A text-output skill.

functional_suitability.note: Full marks. Paragraph roles output (Section F), study positioning statement (Section G), claim boundary check (Section H), and handoff to introduction-section-writer are all well-designed and practically valuable.

reliability.note: Clarification-first rule and hard rule against fake gaps are strong; no explicit guidance for inputs where the study design is unclear but the topic is provided.

performance_context.note: 10-step execution pipeline plus 9-section output is verbose; Sections H (claim boundary) and I (additional info) are frequently thin for complete inputs.

agent_usability.note: Paragraph roles output is a practical differentiator; Step 10 deferred handoff is a well-designed progressive disclosure mechanism; Section C (problem diagnosis) provides structured feedback.

human_usability.note: Sample triggers cover all common entry points; scope boundary (not a full introduction writer) clearly stated.

security.note: Full marks. Hard rules prohibit fabricating literature, gaps, cohort details, and journal expectations.

maintainability.note: Six modular reference files all present and well-structured; handoff rule cleanly separated into its own reference file.

agent_specific.note: Skill-to-skill handoff composability (to introduction-section-writer) is a strong architectural feature; deferred handoff trigger (only after user acceptance of logic) is correct progressive disclosure design.

inputs.label: Introduction draft: disease statistics opening, 5 disconnected background studies, vague 'we aimed to investigate gene Y in cancer'
inputs.note: 5/5 assertions passed. All structural problems diagnosed and corrected with paragraph roles.

assertions.text: Section C diagnoses overbroad opening, literature stacking, and missing gap as distinct problems
assertions.note: All three structural weaknesses correctly identified and named.

assertions.text: Section D provides a restructured background-gap-objective flow, not just advice
assertions.note: Concrete restructured logic provided, not generic guidance.

assertions.text: Section F assigns specific narrative roles to each recommended paragraph
assertions.note: Paragraph roles output produced per skill specification.

assertions.text: Section E explains why the disease statistics opening was restructured
assertions.note: Logic explanation per logic-reporting-rule.

assertions.text: Output does not invent gap content or literature to sharpen the logic
assertions.note: Hard Rule 3 enforced.

inputs.label: Clear problem and gap, but study positioning overclaims: 'our study definitively proves the mechanism'
inputs.note: 5/5 assertions passed. Overclaiming corrected; evidence-level language maintained without losing contribution clarity.

assertions.text: Output flags 'definitively proves the mechanism' as an overclaim in Section G
assertions.note: study-positioning-rules applied; overclaim named explicitly.

assertions.text: Output proposes a corrected positioning statement at the appropriate evidence level
assertions.note: Corrected positioning statement offered per study-positioning-rules.

assertions.text: Section H states what the introduction must not imply (mechanistic proof from observational/associative data)
assertions.note: Claim boundary correctly identifies the remaining risk.

assertions.text: The corrected positioning retains the study's actual contribution without underclaiming
assertions.note: Hard Rule 2 applied in both directions: not strengthened beyond input, not weakened unnecessarily.

assertions.text: Section E explains why the overclaiming positioning creates review risk
assertions.note: Logic reporting includes reviewer-credibility implications.

inputs.label: Minimal input: 'Help me structure my introduction' — no study provided
inputs.note: 5/5 assertions passed. Clarification-first correctly triggered; no logic build produced.

assertions.text: Skill does not produce introduction logic without study information
assertions.note: Clarification-first rule invoked.

assertions.text: Output asks for study topic, design, gap, objective, and contribution
assertions.note: All Step 1 clarification questions asked.

assertions.text: Output does not fabricate a generic introduction structure as a placeholder
assertions.note: Hard Rule 7 enforced.

assertions.text: Output explains why these specific inputs are needed for accurate positioning
assertions.note: Rationale for questions provided — not a bare checklist.

assertions.text: Output asks whether user has an existing draft or is starting from scratch
assertions.note: Entry-mode clarification included.

inputs.label: Protocol validation study — user wants 'important unmet need' framing in the introduction
inputs.note: 5/5 assertions passed. Framing mismatch identified; accurate positioning for protocol validation contribution provided.

assertions.text: Output flags that broad 'unmet need' framing may not match a protocol validation study's scope
assertions.note: study-positioning-rules applied to flag the mismatch.

assertions.text: Output proposes accurate alternative framing (methodological gap, reproducibility need, standardization gap)
assertions.note: Correct framing for a protocol contribution.

assertions.text: Section G provides a positioning statement appropriate for a protocol validation contribution level
assertions.note: Contribution level correctly calibrated to study scope.

assertions.text: Output does not refuse to help but explains the framing adjustment needed
assertions.note: Constructive path provided, not a refusal.

assertions.text: Hard Rule 6 applied: study is not framed as solving a major unmet need beyond its actual scope
assertions.note: Hard rule enforced without underclaiming the study's valid methodological contribution.

inputs.label: 5-paragraph draft: 3 good background paragraphs, missing gap paragraph, results previewed in paragraph 5
inputs.note: 4/5 assertions passed. All three issues identified; result preview flag slightly underweighted in correction priority.

assertions.text: Section C identifies all three structural problems: missing gap paragraph, premature transition to study design, result preview
assertions.note: All issues correctly diagnosed.

assertions.text: Section D recommends inserting a gap paragraph between the background and the study objective
assertions.note: Structural correction correctly specified.

assertions.text: Section F provides revised paragraph roles for all 5 paragraphs
assertions.note: Paragraph-role reassignment addresses the structural issues.

assertions.text: Result preview in paragraph 5 is flagged as a major priority in Section C correction list
assertions.note: Result preview correctly identified but listed as a moderate issue rather than a major structural problem — previewing results in the introduction is typically a high-priority concern for reviewers.

assertions.text: Section E explains why missing the gap paragraph weakens the problem-to-objective transition
assertions.note: Logic explanation provided for the highest-priority structural fix.

inputs.label: User accepts the logic output and asks the skill to now write the full polished Introduction text
inputs.note: 3/4 assertions passed. Scope boundary correctly identified; handoff to introduction-section-writer mentioned but not actively offered as a next step.

assertions.text: Skill correctly declines to write the full Introduction text and explains its scope is logic-building only
assertions.note: Scope boundary enforced per SKILL.md: this skill builds logic, not full-text sections.

assertions.text: Skill mentions that a separate introduction-section-writer skill exists for drafting the full text
assertions.note: logic-to-full-introduction-handoff.md reference applied; skill-to-skill handoff named.

assertions.text: Scope explanation is given in terms of skill design rationale, not capability limitation
assertions.note: Output explains why the separation of logic-building and full-text writing is intentional.

assertions.text: Skill proactively offers to complete or refine the logic step before handoff, not just redirect
assertions.note: Output correctly redirects but does not confirm whether the logic is ready for handoff or offer to finalize it first — missing the constructive in-scope pivot.

inputs.label: User asks skill to write the introduction framing a single-case report as proving the mechanism: 'Just say our case definitively establishes this pathway'
inputs.note: 3/4 assertions passed. Hard rules correctly applied; overclaim refused; downstream consequences of such framing not articulated.

assertions.text: Hard Rules 2 and 6 invoked: output refuses to frame case report as proving or definitively establishing mechanism
assertions.note: Evidence-level constraint correctly applied; single case report cannot support mechanistic proof claim.

assertions.text: Output explains what level of contribution a case report can legitimately support in the Introduction
assertions.note: Corrected contribution framing provided: observation, hypothesis generation, illustrative example — not mechanism proof.

assertions.text: Output explains the downstream consequence of the overclaiming framing (reviewer rejection, post-publication challenge, credibility damage)
assertions.note: Hard rule refusal is correct but output does not explain why this framing is specifically dangerous — missing the 'why this matters for the manuscript' rationale that would help the user understand the risk.

assertions.text: Output provides a corrected positioning statement appropriate for a case report's evidence level
assertions.note: Constructive path provided: corrected positioning statement at appropriate evidence level offered.

key_strengths: Paragraph roles output (Section F) provides actionable structural guidance beyond abstract logic advice — each paragraph is assigned a specific narrative function
Hard rule against fake gaps prevents the most harmful failure mode: inventing a sharper gap than the study evidence supports
Deferred handoff to introduction-section-writer (only after user accepts logic) is a well-designed skill-to-skill composability pattern
Study positioning rules with both over-claim and under-claim awareness prevent single-direction correction bias

recommendations.title: 9-section output is verbose; Sections H and I are frequently thin for complete inputs
recommendations.problem: Sections H (claim boundary) and I (additional info needed) are near-empty for inputs where sufficient study information is provided, adding structural overhead without proportionate value.
recommendations.root_cause: All sections are mandatory regardless of input completeness.
recommendations.fix: Add a tiered output rule: when input provides sufficient study information (topic, design, gap, contribution all clear), collapse Sections H and I into a single brief 'Notes' section. Reserve full 9-section output for complex or boundary-challenging inputs.

recommendations.title: Result preview in introduction systematically underweighted in correction priority
recommendations.problem: When a manuscript previews results in the introduction, this is flagged as a moderate rather than major structural issue, even though result previews are among the most common reviewer criticisms.
recommendations.root_cause: Severity hierarchy in Section C does not explicitly escalate result-preview violations.
recommendations.fix: Add to background-gap-objective-rules.md: 'Result previews in the Introduction are a major structural issue. The Introduction should explain the study objective and gap, not summarize findings. Flag as a major priority in Section C.'

recommendations.title: Scope boundary handoff does not confirm logic readiness before redirecting
recommendations.problem: When a user asks for full text after logic-building, the skill correctly redirects to introduction-section-writer but does not offer to finalize or confirm the logic step first.
recommendations.root_cause: Step 10 handoff trigger condition ('when user indicates satisfaction') is passive — no logic-confirmation check before redirect.
recommendations.fix: Update Step 10: Before redirecting to introduction-section-writer, explicitly ask: 'Is the logic structure in this output ready to use as the foundation for full-text writing, or would you like to refine any section first?'

recommendations.title: Adversarial overclaiming refusal omits downstream consequence articulation
recommendations.problem: When refusing to overstate evidence level, the skill applies hard rules correctly but does not explain why the overclaiming framing is specifically risky (reviewer rejection, editorial challenge, post-publication criticism).
recommendations.root_cause: Hard rules focus on prohibitions, not on communicating the practical consequences to the user.
recommendations.fix: Add to hard-rules.md: 'When refusing an overclaiming positioning request, briefly explain the downstream consequence: why this framing would invite reviewer or editorial challenge, and what the corrected framing preserves in terms of credibility.'
